This article details the [[Bradford Bulls]] rugby league football club's 2024 season. This is the Bulls' sixth consecutive season in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]].

==Season review==

===May 2023===

[[Hull Kingston Rovers]] announced the signing of second rower [[Ajahni Wallace]] from the Bulls on a 2-year deal from the 2024 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://hullkr.co.uk/news/hull-kr-capture-aj-wallace-on-a-two-year-deal-from-2024|title=HULL KR CAPTURE AJ WALLACE ON A TWO-YEAR DEAL FROM 2024 |work=Hull Kingston Rovers |date=13 May 2023 |accessdate=18 March 2026}}</ref>

===June 2023===

Prop forward [[Josh Johnson (rugby league)|Josh Johnson]] left the club by mutual consent in order to join [[Oldham R.L.F.C.]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/23621143.bradford-bulls-see-josh-johnson-depart-league-1-oldham/|title=Bradford Bulls see Josh Johnson depart for League 1 Oldham |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=29 June 2023 |accessdate=18 March 2026}}</ref>

===July 2023===

[[Bodene Thompson]] announced his retirement from the sport in order to return home to [[New Zealand]] to spend time with his family.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/23628358.bodene-thompson-leaves-bradford-bulls-better-place/?ref=rss|title=Bodene Thompson leaves Bradford Bulls in "better place" |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=3 July 2023 |accessdate=18 March 2026}}</ref>

===August 2023===

Loose forward [[Brad Foster (rugby league)|Brad Foster]] left the club after one season to explore other first-team opportunities.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/23699698.brad-foster-leaves-bradford-bulls-one-season/|title=Brad Foster leaves Bradford Bulls after one season |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=3 August 2023 |accessdate=18 March 2026}}</ref> Winger [[David Foggin-Johnston]] announced that he would be leaving the club at the end of the season to join local side [[Keighley Cougars]] on a 2-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/23731032.winger-leave-bradford-bulls-join-keighley-cougars/|title=Winger to leave Bradford Bulls and join Keighley Cougars |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=18 August 2023 |accessdate=18 March 2026}}</ref>

===September 2023===

Prop forward [[Daniel Okoro]] signed a permanent deal with the Bulls after spending time at the club on loan in the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/bradford-bulls-swoop-for-super-league-forward-on-permanent-deal/|title=Bradford Bulls swoop for Super League forward on permanent deal |work=Total Rugby League |date=13 September 2023 |accessdate=21 March 2026}}</ref> Winger [[Will Oakes]] joined the club on a one-year deal from [[York City Knights]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://bradfordbulls.co.uk/news/welcome-will/|title=WELCOME, WILL! |work=Bradford Bulls |date=26 September 2023 |accessdate=21 March 2026}}</ref>

===October 2023===

Former player [[Sam Hallas]] returned to the club for a third spell as he put pen to paper on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/23834627.bradford-bulls-get-sam-hallas-hunslet-third-spell/|title=Bradford Bulls get Sam Hallas from Hunslet for third spell |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=4 October 2023 |accessdate=18 March 2026}}</ref> [[French people|French]] loose forward [[Jason Baitieri]] announced that he was leaving the Bulls at the end of his contract to return to [[France]] to play for [[Pia Donkeys]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/23849375.jason-baitieri-returns-france-bradford-bulls-stint/|title=Jason Baitieri returns to France after Bradford Bulls stint |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=11 October 2023 |accessdate=18 March 2026}}</ref> Prolific halfback [[Dec Patton]] left the club to join [[RFL Championship]] side [[Swinton Lions]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/23872434.bradford-bulls-see-dec-patton-depart-swinton-lions/|title=Bradford Bulls see Dec Patton depart for Swinton Lions |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=23 October 2023 |accessdate=17 March 2026}}</ref> Prop forward [[Masimbaashe Matongo]] left the Bulls to take some time away from the sport.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/former-hull-fc-prop-masi-9732841|title=Former Hull FC prop Masi Matongo signs for Championship club after break from sport |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=22 November 2024 |accessdate=17 March 2026}}</ref> It was announced in December 2022 that young academy prospect [[George Flanagan Jr]] would depart the Bulls at the end of the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://bradfordbulls.co.uk/news/george-flanagan-jnr-to-depart-the-bulls-in-2024/#:~:text=Bradford%20Bulls%20have%20confirmed%20that,commence%20in%20the%202024%20season.|title=GEORGE FLANAGAN JR TO DEPART THE BULLS IN 2024 |work=Bradford Bulls |date=21 December 2022 |accessdate=18 March 2026}}</ref> Young [[Australians|Australian]] hooker [[Mitch Souter]] signed for the Bulls on a one-year deal from [[Canberra Raiders]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://bradfordbulls.co.uk/news/mitch-souts-the-bulls/|title=MITCH SOUTS THE BULLS! |work=Bradford Bulls |date=26 October 2023 |accessdate=18 March 2026}}</ref> Prop [[Daniel Smith (rugby league)|Daniel Smith]] signed a two-year deal with the club following his release from [[Featherstone Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/super-league-prop-joins-new-club-84462/|title=Super League prop joins new club |work=Serious About RL |date=30 October 2023 |accessdate=18 March 2026}}</ref>

===November 2023===

Veteran back rower [[John Davies (rugby league, born 1991)|John Davies]] signed a one-year deal with the Bulls after spending 8 seasons at [[Featherstone Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/featherstone-rovers-man-john-davies-signs-for-bradford-bulls/|title=Featherstone Rovers man John Davies signs for Bradford Bulls |work=Total Rugby League |date=3 November 2023 |accessdate=17 March 2026}}</ref> It wouldn't take long for Davies to return to his former club as Featherstone announced a pre season friendly against the Bulls for January 7.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/featherstone-rovers-set-to-tackle-bradford-bulls-and-dewsbury-rams-in-first-pre-season-fixtures-4424679|title=Featherstone Rovers set to tackle Bradford Bulls and Dewsbury Rams in first pre-season fixtures |work=Wakefield Express |date=27 November 2023 |accessdate=18 March 2026}}</ref> Hooker [[James Segeyaro]] left Bradford at the end of his contract and signed for [[Illawarra Rugby League]] side [[Western Suburbs Red Devils]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/23920097.hooker-leaves-bradford-bulls-join-australian-side/|title=Hooker leaves Bradford Bulls to join Australian side |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=13 November 2023 |accessdate=18 March 2026}}</ref> Young halfback [[Myles Lawford]] left the Bulls at the end of his contract in order to sign for [[Wakefield Trinity]] with the Bulls receiving some compensation.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://bradfordbulls.co.uk/news/lawford-to-join-wakefield-trinity/|title=LAWFORD TO JOIN WAKEFIELD TRINITY |work=Bradford Bulls |date=13 November 2023 |accessdate=18 March 2026}}</ref>

===December 2023===

[[Huddersfield Giants]] fullback [[Aiden McGowan]] signed for the club on a season long loan in order to get game time.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://bradfordbulls.co.uk/news/mcgowan-joins-on-season-long-loan/|title=MCGOWAN JOINS ON SEASON LONG LOAN |work=Bradford Bulls |date=18 December 2023 |accessdate=21 March 2026}}</ref> Academy graduate [[Marcus Green (rugby league)|Marcus Green]] was released by mutual consent and signed with [[Midlands Hurricanes]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://bradfordbulls.co.uk/news/green-released-by-mutual-consent/|title=GREEN RELEASED BY MUTUAL CONSENT |work=Bradford Bulls |date=23 December 2023 |accessdate=18 March 2026}}</ref> The Bulls kicked off pre-season with a tight 24–16 win over local rivals [[Halifax Panthers]] with [[Chester Butler (rugby league)|Chester Butler]], [[Brad England]], [[George Flanagan]] and [[Jayden Myers]] grabbing the tries and [[Jordan Lilley]] kicking three goals.

===January 2024===

Bradford continued their season preparations with a 16–12 loss to [[Featherstone Rovers]]. Winger [[Jorge Taufua]] crossed for two tries whist fellow winger [[Ben Blackmore]] scored the other one. [[Hull F.C.]] prepared for their [[Super League]] season by sending a young squad to face the Bulls in a friendly.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hullfc.com/blog/2024/01/14/team-news-bradford-bulls-vs-hull-fc/|title=Team News: Bradford Bulls vs Hull FC |work=Hull FC |date=14 January 2024 |accessdate=17 March 2026}}</ref> The Bulls beat [[Hull FC]] 34–32 with both teams playing quite young sides, senior players such as [[Joe Arundel]] and [[Brad England]] scored for the Bulls, with pack man [[Daniel Smith (rugby league)|Daniel Smith]] scoring two. It was announced that the Bulls would face arch rivals [[Leeds Rhinos]] in a pre season friendly for the Keith Howard Foundation Trophy on 28 February.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://bradfordbulls.co.uk/news/bulls-to-face-rhinos-for-keith-howard-foundation-trophy/|title=BULLS TO FACE RHINOS FOR KEITH HOWARD FOUNDATION TROPHY |work=Bradford Bulls |date=15 January 2024 |accessdate=17 March 2026}}</ref> Bradford accepted a transfer request from second rower Brad England so he could join Featherstone Rovers with immediate effect.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://bradfordbulls.co.uk/news/bulls-confirm-england-departure/|title=BULLS COMFIRM ENGLAND DEPARTURE |work=Bradford Bulls |date=24 January 2024 |accessdate=5 April 2026}}</ref> After impressing in pre-season, young trialist prop [[Elliot Peposhi]] signed a one-year deal with the club.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/24076636.bradford-bulls-make-move-sign-eliot-peposhi-permanently/|title=Bradford Bulls make move to sign Eliot Peposhi permanently |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=26 January 2024 |accessdate=21 March 2026}}</ref> Bradford rounded off pre-season with a comprehensive 34-8 win over a youthful Leeds side with hooker [[Mitch Souter]] taking the headlines with two tries.

===February 2024===

The Bulls kicked off their season with a 40-4 win in the [[2024 RFL 1895 Cup]] against [[Dewsbury Rams]] and then backed this performance up in the [[2024 Challenge Cup]] by beating [[North Wales Crusaders]] 48-2 with [[George Flanagan]] grabbing a brace of tries. A 26-18 win over local rivals [[Keighley Cougars]] ensured the Bulls kept their 100% winning record to start the season. Bradford exited the Challenge Cup with a 26-12 loss to [[Widnes Vikings]]. With injuries mounting up the Bulls announced the signing of centre [[Keanan Brand]] from [[Leigh Leopards]] on a two week loan as well as hooker [[Corey Johnson (rugby league)|Corey Johnson]] from [[Leeds Rhinos]] on a season long loan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/24152154.keanan-brand-joins-bradford-bulls-leigh-leopards/|title=Keanan Brand joins Bradford Bulls from Leigh Leopards |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=29 February 2024 |accessdate=5 April 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-corey-johnson-joins-bradford-bulls-on-long-term-loan-4538673|title=Leeds Rhinos' Corey Johnson joins Bradford Bulls on long-term loan |work=Yorkshire Evening Post |date=29 February 2024 |accessdate=5 April 2026}}</ref>

===March 2024===

Bradford started March by progressing to the [[2024 RFL 1895 Cup]] quarter-finals by beating [[Swinton Lions]] 21-12. Young Australian second rower [[Zac Fulton]] (grandson of Australian [[Immortals (rugby league)|Immortal]] [[Bob Fulton]]) on a one year deal from [[NRL]] side [[Manly Sea Eagles]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://bradfordbulls.co.uk/news/bulls-sign-aussie-ace-fulton/|title=BULLS SIGN AUSSIE ACE FULTON! |work=Bradford Bulls |date=5 March 2024 |accessdate=5 April 2026}}</ref> Ahead of the Championship opener the Bulls announced the signing of young prop [[Lucas Green (rugby league)|Lucas Green]] from [[Warrington Wolves]] and young second-row [[Harvey Wilson (rugby league)|Harvey Wilson]] from [[Wigan Warriors]] both on an initial one month loan deals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/24181867.bradford-bulls-loan-warrington-wigan-youngsters/|title=Bradford Bulls loan in Warrington and Wigan youngsters |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=13 March 2024 |accessdate=5 April 2026}}</ref> The Bulls kicked off the [[2024 RFL Championship]] season with a heavy 42-12 loss to favourites [[Wakefield Trinity]]. The Bulls signed [[North Sydney Bears]] hooker [[Tyran Ott]] on a one year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/bradford-bulls-draft-australian-hooker-28722599|title=Bradford Bulls draft in Australian hooker as high-profile recruitment continues |work=Yorkshire Live |date=15 March 2024 |accessdate=5 April 2026}}</ref> After losing [[Jorge Taufua]] to suspension, the Bulls brought in young winger [[Connor Carr]] from [[Huddersfield Giants]] on a two week loan deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.giantsrl.com/article/8798/connor-carr-to-join-bradford-on-loan|title=CONNOR CARR TO JOIN BRADFORD ON-LOAN |work=Huddersfield Giants |date=26 March 2024 |accessdate=5 April 2026}}</ref> Young prop [[Max Wood]] joined the Bulls on a two week loan deal from [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/wolvesnews/24213180.max-wood-joins-bradford-bulls-loan-warrington-wolves/|title=Max Wood joins Bradford Bulls on loan from Warrington Wolves |work=Warrington Guardian |date=16 March 2024 |accessdate=5 April 2026}}</ref> Loan signing [[Keanan Brand]] returned to his parent club [[Leigh Leopards]] after suffering an MCL injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/24220501.leigh-leopards-star-keanan-brand-suffers-knee-injury/#:~:text=BRADFORD%20Bulls%20and%20Super%20League,final%20of%20the%201895%20Cup.|title=Leigh Leopards star Keanan Brand suffers knee injury |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=30 March 2024 |accessdate=5 April 2026}}</ref> Bradford rounded off the month by getting their first win in the Championship by beating local rivals [[Halifax Panthers]] 29-10 with debutant [[Zac Fulton]] scoring.

===April 2024===

The Bulls announced that former loanee [[Fenton Rogers]] has rejoined the club on loan from [[Huddersfield Giants]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/24232650.huddersfield-giants-star-fenton-rogers-joins-bradford-bulls/|title=Huddersfield Giants star Fenton Rogers joins Bradford Bulls |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=4 April 2024 |accessdate=6 April 2026}}</ref> [[Warrington Wolves]] announced the signing of Bulls forward [[Daniel Okoro]] with immediate effect.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68736557|title=Daniel Okoro: Warrington Wolves sign Bradford Bulls prop until 2027 |work=BBC Sport |date=4 April 2024 |accessdate=6 April 2026}}</ref> Bradford kicked off April by beating [[Featherstone Rovers]] 24-14, the first time the Bulls had won at [[Post Office Road]] since 2015. The Bulls backed this up with a 19-12 win over [[Toulouse Olympique]] which took Bradford up to 4th in the league. However the following week the Bulls suffered a 25-14 loss to [[York Knights]]. Young [[Wigan Warriors]] winger [[Jacob Douglas (rugby league)|Jacob Douglas]] signed for the club on a two week loan deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/24280644.bradford-bulls-grateful-get-wigan-warriors-winger-loan/|title=Bradford Bulls grateful to get Wigan Warriors winger on loan |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=26 April 2024 |accessdate=7 April 2026}}</ref> The Bulls finished the month off with a late 14-13 defeat to [[Widnes Vikings]].

===May 2024===

The Bulls started the month off well with a 38-12 win over [[Swinton Lions]] with centre [[Kieran Gill]] grabbing the headlines with two tries. Veteran hooker [[George Flanagan]] signed for local rivals [[Keighley Cougars]] on a season long loan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://keighleycougars.uk/flanagan-joins-on-loan-from-bulls|title=FLANAGAN JOINS ON LOAN FROM BULLS |work=Keighley Cougars |date=7 May 2024 |accessdate=7 April 2026}}</ref> Bradford exited the [[2024 RFL 1895 Cup]] at the semi final stage by losing 40-14 to eventual winners [[Wakefield Trinity]]. The Bulls got back to winning ways with a 38-12 victory over [[Swinton Lions]]. [[Hull F.C.]] wingers [[Liam Tindall]] and [[Jayden Okunbor]] joined the club on short term loans.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/24309621.hull-fc-winger-liam-tindall-joins-bradford-bulls-loan/|title=Hull FC winger Liam Tindall joins Bradford Bulls on loan |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=9 May 2024 |accessdate=7 April 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://bradfordbulls.co.uk/news/former-nrl-star-okunbor-joins-on-loan/|title=FORMER NRL STAR OKUNBOR JOINS ON LOAN |work=Bradford Bulls |date=10 May 2024 |accessdate=7 April 2026}}</ref> Okunbor scored a try on debut as the Bulls beat [[Sheffield Eagles]] 28-10. Winger [[Ben Blackmore]] asked the club for a release due to work reasons before subsequently signing for [[Keighley Cougars]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/24341578.bradford-bulls-see-ben-blackmore-depart-due-work-reasons/|title=Bradford Bulls see Ben Blackmore depart due to work reasons |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=23 May 2024 |accessdate=7 April 2026}}</ref> The Bulls rounded off May with a narrow 21-20 loss away to [[Batley Bulldogs]].

===June 2024===

Bradford beat [[Barrow Raiders]] 36-24 to start the month off. Australian winger [[Max Lehmann (rugby)|Max Lehmann]] signed for the club from [[Brisbane Tigers]] on a deal for the rest of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/24387059.bradford-bulls-snap-brisbane-tigers-talent-max-lehmann/|title=Bradford Bulls snap up Brisbane Tigers talent Max Lehmann |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=14 June 2024 |accessdate=12 April 2026}}</ref> They backed this up with a 36-18 win over [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.]]. Australian prop forward [[Logan Bayliss-Brow]] joined the club on a two and half year deal from [[Queensland Cup]] side [[Redcliffe Dolphins]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://bradfordbulls.co.uk/news/lbb-joins-bb/#:~:text=Bradford%20Bulls%20are%20delighted%20to,Queensland%20Cup%20outfit%20Redcliffe%20Dolphins.|title=LBB JOINS BB! |work=Bradford Bulls |date=16 June 2024 |accessdate=12 April 2026}}</ref> The Bulls announced the signing of [[Hull F.C.]] prop forward [[Franklin Pele]] on a deal until the end of the 2024 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c0335xr6ze8o|title=Prop Pele joins Bradford after Hull FC release |work=BBC Sport |date=19 June 2024 |accessdate=12 April 2026}}</ref> Bradford continued their winning streak with a 38-12 victory over [[Dewsbury Rams]].

===July 2024===

The Bulls announced the signing of prop forward [[Nathan Mason]] on a permanent 18 month contract from [[Illawarra Rugby League]] side [[Corrimal Cougars]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/24423652.bradford-bulls-get-forward-nathan-mason-back-australia/|title=Bradford Bulls get forward Nathan Mason back from Australia |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=1 July 2024 |accessdate=12 April 2026}}</ref> Loan signing [[Jayden Okunbor]] returned to the club to sign a permanent deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hullfc.com/blog/2024/07/05/okunbor-to-rejoin-bulls-on-permanent-basis/|title=Okunbor To Rejoin Bulls On Permanent Basis |work=Hull FC |date=5 July 2024 |accessdate=12 April 2026}}</ref> Bradford picked up their first point away at [[Toulouse Olympique]] since 2019 with a hard fought 12-12 draw. The following week, Bradford lost 14-2 to league leaders [[Wakefield Trinity]]. Young prop [[Harvey Makin]] joined the club on loan from [[Wigan Warriors]] on an initial one-month deal which was subsequently extended to the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-rising-prop-harvey-makin-heads-back-to-championship-on-loan-4709673|title=Wigan Warriors rising prop Harvey Makin heads back to Championship on loan |work=Wigan Today |date=19 July 2024 |accessdate=13 April 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/24495230.bradford-bulls-extend-loan-wigan-warriors-harvey-makin/|title=Bradford Bulls extend loan of Wigan Warriors' Harvey Makin |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=3 August 2024 |accessdate=13 April 2026}}</ref> The Bulls got back to winning ways with a 36-28 win over [[York Knights]]. Due to 11 injuries to the first team squad, the Bulls signed [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] prop [[Zach Fishwick]] on a two week loan deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/24478557.bradford-bulls-11-widnes-vikings-teen-joins/|title=Bradford Bulls have 11 out for Widnes Vikings as teen joins |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=27 July 2024 |accessdate=13 April 2026}}</ref> Bradford rounded off the month with a disappointing 25-6 loss to [[Widnes Vikings]]. The Bulls announced the signing of former halfback [[Jarrod Sammut]] on a loan deal for the rest of the season from [[Workington Town]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://bradfordbulls.co.uk/news/bulls-re-sign-sammut/|title=BULLS RE-SIGN SAMMUT |work=Bradford Bulls |date=29 July 2024 |accessdate=13 April 2026}}</ref>

===August 2024===

Young [[Leeds Rhinos]] winger [[Jack Smith (rugby league)|Jack Smith]] joined the club on loan for the rest of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://bradfordbulls.co.uk/news/bulls-sign-smith-on-loan-from-rhinos/|title=BULLS SIGN SMITH ON LOAN FROM RHINOS |work=Bradford Bulls |date=1 August 2024 |accessdate=13 April 2026}}</ref> Academy product [[Jacob Bateman]] joined [[Cornwall R.L.F.C.]] on a loan deal for the rest of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cxx2x4x2r7xo|title=Bradford Bulls duo join Cornwall on loan |work=BBC Sport |date=2 August 2024 |accessdate=13 April 2026}}</ref> With an injury crisis ongoing the Bulls brought in [[Wakefield Trinity]] center [[Romain Franco]] on loan for the rest of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/24493717.bradford-bulls-finally-deals-done-six-mad-months/|title=Bradford Bulls finally have deals done after six mad months |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=4 August 2024 |accessdate=17 April 2026}}</ref> The Bulls kicked off the month with a 24-24 draw away to [[Barrow Raiders]], however the following week, Bradford had a comprehensive 58-0 win over [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.]] with [[Keven Appo]] and [[Harvey Makin]] scoring two tries a piece. A last minute 22-21 loss to [[Featherstone Rovers]] was followed with a response against [[Doncaster R.L.F.C]] as the Bulls came away 18-4 winners. To round the month off, Bradford beat [[Dewsbury Rams]] in a 54-0 rout with Franco and [[Franklin Pele]] scoring two tries each.

==Milestones==
* '''1985 Cup Round 2:''' [[Aiden McGowan]], [[John Davies (rugby league, born 1991)|John Davies]] and [[Mitch Souter]] made their debuts for the Bulls.
* '''1985 Cup Round 2:''' [[Aiden McGowan]] and [[Mitch Souter]] scored their 1st try for the Bulls.
* '''CCR3:''' [[Daniel Smith (rugby league)|Daniel Smith]] and [[Elliot Peposhi]] made their debuts for the Bulls.
* '''CCR3:''' [[Eribe Doro]] scored his 1st try for the Bulls.
* '''1985 Cup QF:''' [[Keanan Brand]] made his debut for the Bulls.
* '''Round 1:''' [[Lucas Green (rugby league)|Lucas Green]] and [[Harvey Wilson (rugby league)|Harvey Wilson]] made their debuts for the Bulls.
* '''Round 2:''' [[Zac Fulton]], [[Tyran Ott]], [[Connor Carr]] and [[Max Wood]] made their debuts for the Bulls.
* '''Round 2:''' [[Zac Fulton]] and [[Max Wood]] scored their 1st try for the Bulls.
* '''Round 2:''' [[Jordan Lilley]] kicked his 100th goal and 10th drop goal for the Bulls.
* '''Round 2:''' [[Jordan Lilley]] reached 300 points for the Bulls.
